INSPECTION FILE Restaurant client can save the link/PDF with service reports. NEXT ACTION Photos, open items, and the next service step stay clear. NEXT STEP Clear the blocked area or confirm the next visit window. WHY VENDORS PAY The customer can understand the job and save the record for files. WHAT CHANGES AFTER EACH VISIT The customer should not need a callback to understand the job. The visit ends with one readable service report that explains what was done, what stayed open, and what the customer needs to do next. CUSTOMER SEES One branded service report link/PDF they can save with restaurant files. VENDOR GETS A same-day service report that makes the job easier to trust, file, and defend. WHY VENDORS BUY IT The first value is fewer callbacks and a stronger premium report. A service report instead of raw note clutter Less office time rewriting crew photos into customer explanations Blocked access and open items made visible without a follow-up explanation call Premium service looks premium the moment the service report lands A public sample link that lets vendors show quality before account creation Where the money leaks without it Existing-customer value is not abstract. Money leaks when the customer has to decode the job after the crew leaves. POST-VISIT TRUST RIGHT AFTER SERVICE Most vendors still make the customer guess what actually happened. Loose photos and note fragments force the customer to decode the job or call back for clarification. WHEN PRICE GETS COMPARED If the report feels sloppy, premium service feels overpriced. A branded service report link and PDF make the work look serious before anyone questions the price or the quality of the visit. WHAT GOES INSIDE One clean service report surface with photos, open items, and next action. TODAY'S RESULT What
data.title: Branded Service Reports for Hood Cleaning Jobs | KitchenPermit

buttons.text: HD-01 · STEP 01 Hood canopy Completed Degreased and wiped accessible hood interior surfaces.
FL-01 · STEP 02 Filter bank Completed Removed, cleaned, checked, and returned to service.
PL-01 · STEP 03 Plenum / duct path Completed Reachable path cleaned and documented in service scope.
DK-02 · STEP 04 Rear duct access Inaccessible Not completed: Stored equipment blocked the rear duct access panel during service. This area remains tied to the recorded access item
RF-01 · STEP 05 Rooftop fan area Recorded Visible rooftop and containment conditions stay on record so the service team can answer follow-up questions.

data.textStart: KITCHENPERMIT HOME SERVICE REPORT SAMPLE RESOURCES PRICING ACCOUNT LOGIN Company Reports LEGAL Terms of Service Last updated: May 13, 2026. These terms describe the rules for usingKitchenPermit and its paid subscription features. 1. Service KitchenPermit provides software that helps kitchen exhaust and hood cleaning companies create customer-facing service report links and PDF-ready records. We do not provide hood cleaning, inspection, repair, or field services. 2. Accounts and Access Some features may require an account and an active subscription. You are responsible for keeping account credentials secure and for the accuracy of company, job, photo, and report information entered into the product. 3. Customer Reports Reports are generated from information supplied by users. You are responsible for reviewing reports before sending them to customers, inspectors, or other third parties. The product does not replace professional judgment, code compliance review, or legal advice. 4. Subscriptions Paid subscriptions are billed through our payment provider. Subscription access may continue while payment remains active and may be limited, suspended, or cancelled if payment fails or the subscription is cancelled. 5. Acceptable Use You may not use KitchenPermit to upload unlawful content, infringing content, malware, deceptive reports, or content that violates third-party rights. We may remove content or restrict access when necessary to protect the service, customers, or legal compliance. 6. Availability We aim to keep the service reliable, but we do not guarantee uninterrupted access. Features may change as the product evolves. 7. Limitation of Liability To the maximum extent permitted by law, KitchenPermit is provided as-is and we are not liable for indirect, incidental, special, consequential, or punitive damages arising from use of the service. 8. Contact Questions about these terms can be sent to support@kitchenpermit.com. FOR YOUR COMPANY If the sample works, see how it looks under your company name. h2: Report Photos and Service Report Links
data.textStart: KITCHENPERMIT HOME SERVICE REPORT SAMPLE RESOURCES PRICING ACCOUNT LOGIN Company Reports LEGAL Privacy Policy Last updated: May 13, 2026. This policy explains how KitchenPermit handles information for its SaaS report builder and hosted report links. Information We Collect We collect account information, company profile details, billing status information, report content, uploaded service photos, and technical information needed to operate and secure the product. How We Use Information We use information to provide report builder features, host customer report links, generate PDF-ready records, maintain report history, process support requests, prevent abuse, and manage subscriptions. Payment Data Payments are processed by our payment provider. We do not store full card numbers or sensitive card authentication details on our servers. Report Photos and Service Report Links Uploaded photos and report data are used to create customer-facing report links and records. Users are responsible for ensuring they have the right to upload and share this information. Service Providers We may use infrastructure, database, object storage, analytics, email, and payment providers to operate the service. These providers process information only as needed to deliver their services to us. Retention Free report links may expire after a limited period. Paid account records may be retained while the account or subscription is active, unless deletion is requested or required by law. Contact Privacy questions can be sent to support@kitchenpermit.com. FOR YOUR COMPANY If the sample works, see how it looks under your company name. SET UP COMPANY REPORTS KITCHENPERMIT Branded hood service report links and PDFs for hood cleaning companies. Turn field photos, open items, and next steps into a restaurant-ready report after every visit. data.textStart: KITCHENPERMIT HOME SERVICE REPORT SAMPLE RESOURCES PRICING ACCOUNT LOGIN Company Reports LEGAL Refund Policy Last updated: May 13, 2026. This policy explains how subscription cancellations and refund requests are handled. Subscription Refunds If you believe a charge was made in error, contact us within 14 days of the charge. Refunds are reviewed case by case based on account activity, product access, and the nature of the request. Cancellations You may cancel a subscription to stop future renewals. Cancellation does not automatically refund prior subscription periods that have already been billed. Free Builder The free builder is available for evaluation without payment and does not create a paid billing relationship. Design Help Optional design help or custom setup work may have separate terms based on the scope confirmed before work begins. How to Request Help Send refund or billing questions to support@kitchenpermit.com with your account email and charge details. h2: How to use Hood cleaning service report template
Questions this page should answer before the vendor clicks.
Use this page before the product ask
data.textStart: KITCHENPERMIT HOME SERVICE REPORT SAMPLE RESOURCES PRICING ACCOUNT LOGIN Company Reports TEMPLATE Hood cleaning service report template Use this structure when a restaurant needs one plain-English record after a hood cleaning visit, instead of loose photos, technician shorthand, and a separate follow-up email. BUILD A FREE REPORT See sample START WITH THE SERVICE RESULT Lead with what was completed today, then separate any blocked access, condition notes, or follow-up work so the customer does not have to decode the visit. ATTACH PROOF TO THE RIGHT AREA Group photos by hood canopy, filters, duct or access point, fan area, grease path, and service label so the record reads like an inspection file. END WITH THE NEXT ACTION A report should close with the next service window, an open access request, or a clear customer action instead of ending with a generic thank-you note. WORKFLOW How to use Hood cleaning service report template 01 Start with the service result Lead with what was completed today, then separate any blocked access, condition notes, or follow-up work so the customer does not have to decode the visit. 02 Attach proof to the right area Group photos by hood canopy, filters, duct or access point, fan area, grease path, and service label so the record reads like an inspection file. 03 End with the next action A report should close with the next service window, an open access request, or a clear customer action instead of ending with a generic thank-you note. SEARCH INTENT Queries this page should answer Hood cleaning service report template Hood cleaning service report template example Hood cleaning service report template template CHECKLIST Minimum fields Customer or property name Service date and technician or crew reference Completed work summary Before and after photo groups Open items or blocked access Next service window or customer action Company contact and PDF copy STRUCTURE Report outline JOB DETAILS Location, date, crew, service type, and reference number. TODAY'S RESULT What was cleaned and what the customer can consider complete. PHOTO EVIDENCE Before and after photos grouped by system area. OPEN ITEMS Blocked access, unreachable areas, or condition records. NEXT STEP Recommended service window or customer action. SAVED RECORD Customer link and PDF copy for restaurant files.
